front: Grep vs Glob — which finds what?
back: Grep searches file CONTENTS; Glob finds files by NAME/PATH pattern. Never use filename search to find code references inside files.

front: When does plan mode earn its overhead?
back: Multi-file changes, architectural choices, migrations/breaking changes, stakeholder approval needed, or read-only exploration first. Tiny clear edits → direct execution.

front: Plan mode vs extended thinking — what problem does each solve?
back: Plan mode = workflow control (gate thinking→doing behind human review). Extended thinking/effort = reasoning quality on hard problems. Different mechanisms; combinable.
--continue: latest conversation here, no prompt. --resume: pick a specific session. --session-id: stable UUID for programmatic use. --fork-session: branch a new transcript from an existing one.

front: Comparing two implementation approaches from the same starting state — what's the full setup?
back: Fork the session (transcripts diverge independently) AND use separate git worktrees (file changes don't collide). Sessions persist conversation, not filesystem state.

No — CLAUDE.md, rules, and auto memory are context (soft guidance). Behavior that must always hold — blocked paths, destructive-command approval, formatters — needs PreToolUse hooks or permissions.deny.

front: How do CLAUDE.md files load across the directory tree?
back: Working dir + ancestors: fully at launch (broader scopes first). Subdirectories: on demand when files there are read. @imports: expanded at launch — they organize, they don't save tokens.

front: CLAUDE.md vs .claude/rules/ vs skills vs hooks — the rule of thumb?
back: Every-session fact → CLAUDE.md. Area-specific → rules with paths: globs. Deliberate multi-step procedure → skill (or slash command if human-invoked only). Must-run-deterministically → hook.

front: Auto memory: what loads at session start, and where does it live?
back: First 200 lines or 25KB of MEMORY.md; topic files load on demand. Stored per project at ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory/ — machine-local, shared across the repo's worktrees, never synced.

front: Claude ignores a rule you wrote. First diagnostic?
back: /memory — confirm the file is actually loaded for this working directory. If it isn't loading, wording changes can't help. Deeper: the InstructionsLoaded hook logs what loaded, when, why.

front: Task-scoped vs path-scoped instructions — why aren't they interchangeable?
back: Task-scoped (reviews, releases) → slash command/skill/subagent, invoked deliberately. Path-scoped (API dir, tests) → rules with paths:, triggered by file reads. A review checklist in paths-rules fires on every touch of those files, review or not.
